[discovered-from: opencodehx-000]

The current CLI/session path has fake-provider, mock AI SDK, first live registry validation, retries, compaction, and first tool-call continuation evidence, but full live OpenCode session behavior remains partial.
acceptance_criteria: Haxe-owned and/or differential fixtures cover live provider session loops, model-emitted tool calls, tool-result continuation, permission prompts, abort/retry/compaction persistence, session resume/continue, and credential-backed success paths where safe; docs and matrix rows for session/llm/prompt/compaction/retry are updated; generated TS strict-checks and smoke gates pass.
notes:
Partial evidence added: ported the pure upstream session.llm.hasToolCalls helper as src/opencodehx/session/SessionLlm.hx over typed AI SDK prompt messages, with SessionProcessorSmoke coverage for empty messages, string content, text-only parts, tool-call, tool-result, and mixed content. Updated docs/session-processor-one-turn.md plus the generated matrix source/CSV. Remaining scope: credential-backed success, live provider cancellation, retry scheduling, and broader prompt/message-history construction.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.compatibilityTools now ports the upstream LiteLLM/GitHub Copilot _noop tool compatibility rule. SessionProcessorSmoke covers provider-id, api-id, explicit litellmProxy, and GitHub Copilot positive cases plus normal-provider, no-history-tool, and active-tool negative cases. Generated SessionLlm.ts stays strict and avoids unsafeCast in this helper.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.resolveTools now ports the upstream active-tool filtering rule. SessionProcessorSmoke covers prompt-level permission re-enabling a tool denied by the agent, user.tools false hiding a tool, agent deny removal, and prompt deny overriding agent allow. Generated SessionLlm.ts assigns selected tools through a null guard with no unsafeCast in the helper.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.requestHeaders now ports the upstream streaming request header assembly rule. SessionProcessorSmoke covers default non-opencode session affinity/User-Agent headers, optional parent session IDs, opencode provider metadata headers, model header overrides, and plugin/chat header overrides. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits this helper without any/unsafeCast.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.composeSystem, finalizeSystemTransform, and requestMessages now port the upstream system-message assembly seam. SessionProcessorSmoke covers provider fallback filtering, agent prompt replacement, blank system preservation, plugin transform rejoin behavior, changed-header pass-through, system-message prepending, and OpenAI OAuth/workflow skips. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits these helpers without any/unsafeCast.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.activeToolNames and workflowPreapprovedTools now port upstream streamText tool-name filtering and GitLab workflow preapproval selection. SessionProcessorSmoke covers invalid-tool exclusion, ask-rule exclusion, allow/deny preapproval, prompt ask overriding an agent allow, and no-rule inclusion. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits these helpers without any/unsafeCast.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.repairToolCall now ports the upstream experimental_repairToolCall decision. SessionProcessorSmoke covers case-only repair to an existing lower-case tool, invalid-tool fallback with JSON error payload, call-id/input preservation, and the lower-case failed-call fallback branch. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits the helper without any/unsafeCast.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.requestOptions now ports upstream request-option assembly for session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers provider-transform base options, providerOptions setCacheKey behavior, model/agent/variant merge order including nested records, small-option base with variant skip, missing variant fallback, and OpenAI OAuth instructions. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; generated any is confined to the documented ProviderOptions SDK passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.requestParams now ports upstream chat.params default assembly for session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers agent temperature/topP overrides, provider transform temperature/topP/topK fallbacks, temperature capability suppression, maxOutputTokens, and provider-options forwarding. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits the params helper without unsafeCast; remaining any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.workflowApprovalNames/workflowApprovalPatterns/workflowAlreadyApproved/rememberWorkflowApproval now port the pure GitLab workflow approval shaping from upstream session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers unique approval names, title/name extraction from JSON args, invalid JSON fallback, falsey non-null title handling, already-approved duplicate handling, approval set updates, and upstream preapproved-list append semantics. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; workflow args parse through genes.ts.Unknown/UnknownNarrow, with remaining generated any confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.workflowUnknownToolResult/workflowToolExecutionResult/workflowToolExecutionError now port the pure GitLab workflow tool-executor result shaping from upstream session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers unknown-tool errors, string tool result pass-through, object output/title/metadata extraction, JSON fallback for objects without output, and caught Error message formatting. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; workflow result values remain genes.ts.Unknown at the boundary, with remaining generated any confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.transformStreamPrompt now ports the pure AI SDK middleware branch from upstream session.llm that applies ProviderTransform.message only for stream requests. SessionProcessorSmoke covers non-stream prompts staying untouched and stream prompts applying Anthropic empty-content filtering through ProviderTransform.message. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; remaining generated any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.telemetryOptions now ports the pure experimental_telemetry object assembly from upstream session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers absent OpenTelemetry enablement, functionId session.llm, username fallback to unknown, session metadata, enabled telemetry, and unknown tracer passthrough. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; telemetry tracer remains unknown/undefined and remaining generated any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.streamFailureError now ports the upstream Stream.fromAsyncIterable error mapping from session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers native Error passthrough plus primitive string/number failures becoming new Error(String(value)) messages. Generated SessionLlm.ts emits an instanceof Error branch and new Error fallback with no unsafeCast; the source cast is documented at the Std.isOfType boundary, and remaining generated any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.streamTextOptions now ports the pure streamText option assembly from upstream session.llm for provider-option routing, active tool filtering, tool choice, max output tokens, headers, and retry defaulting. SessionProcessorSmoke covers gateway providerOptions routing, invalid-tool exclusion from activeTools, explicit toolChoice, explicit retries, retry default 0, and header passthrough. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; remaining generated any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: SessionLlm.workflowModelState now ports the pure GitLab workflow model state setup from upstream session.llm. SessionProcessorSmoke covers workflow sessionID assignment, system prompt joining, and sessionPreapprovedTools derived from the same last-match permission rules as workflowPreapprovedTools. Generated SessionLlm.ts has no unsafeCast; remaining generated any stays confined to the documented ProviderOptions passthrough merge boundary.
Additional partial evidence: AiSdkProvider.stream now accepts public AI SDK ModelMessage prompt input through a typed AiModelMessages bridge while preserving provider-level LanguageModelV3 prompt records. SessionProcessor.runAiSdk uses SessionLlm.requestModelMessages for initial and continuation streamText calls, and SessionProcessorSmoke asserts the mock language model receives system/user prompt evidence plus stable tool-result continuation fragments. Generated TypeScript typechecks without casting provider prompt DTOs into the public ModelMessage union.
Additional partial evidence: SessionProcessor.runAiSdk continuation calls now send public AI SDK ModelMessage history with the original system/user prompt, an assistant ToolCallPart, and a matching tool ToolResultPart instead of a synthetic prose continuation prompt. AiModelMessages owns the public ModelMessage/ToolCallPart/ToolResultPart bridge, the raw @:ts.type audit documents the new SDK-owned aliases, and SessionProcessorSmoke asserts the converted provider prompt contains system/user/assistant-tool-call/tool-result messages with the read-tool output.
Additional partial evidence: AI SDK tool-result continuations now accumulate every completed tool turn in public ModelMessage history. SessionProcessor keeps a typed AiModelToolResultTurn list across repeated continuations, AiModelMessages emits system/user plus each assistant ToolCallPart and matching tool ToolResultPart, and SessionProcessorSmoke asserts the two-tool mock's final provider prompt contains both prior read results before the final answer.
Additional partial evidence: SessionProcessor.runAiSdk now propagates AI SDK provider stream errors and aborts at the session boundary. The async processor passes abortStreamImmediately through to AiSdkProvider.stream, maps StreamError/StreamAbort into session events, marks aborted stream results and assistant error/text consistently, and SessionProcessorSmoke covers provider error finish plus aborted stream result/assistant state.
Additional partial evidence: SessionProcessor.runAiSdk continuation controls now have explicit smoke coverage. The async AI SDK harness verifies continueAfterToolResult=false executes the model-emitted tool once without a follow-up streamText call, and maxToolContinuations=1 caps a multi-tool model after the first continuation while preserving the executed tool-result state. Docs and the generated parity matrix now call out explicit opt-out/max-continuation cap evidence.
Additional partial evidence: SessionProcessor.runAiSdk now has store-backed recovery coverage on the async AI SDK path. SessionProcessorSmoke persists a credential-free mock AI SDK turn into SqliteSessionStore, recovers it through SessionProcessor.recover, and verifies the recovered session directory, message count, and assistant text. Docs and the generated parity matrix now distinguish store-backed recovery evidence from later interactive resume/continue wiring.
Additional partial evidence and fix: AI SDK store-backed recovery now covers persisted tool turns, not only text turns. SessionProcessorSmoke persists a model-emitted read tool call, recovers it through SessionProcessor.recover, and verifies the recovered assistant step-start/tool/text/step-finish ordering. This exposed and fixed SqliteSessionStore.hydrate ordering: recovered parts now sort by message_id, time_created, id instead of message_id, id so persisted part offsets preserve assistant part order.
Additional partial evidence: SessionPersistenceSmoke now covers store-backed tool export ordering and sanitized export behavior. The fixture persists a read tool turn, exports it through SessionExport, verifies step-start/tool/text/step-finish order plus callID preservation, and checks sanitized export redacts assistant text while preserving tool call identity. Docs and the generated parity matrix now call out text/tool export ordering and sanitization evidence.
Additional partial evidence and privacy fix: sanitized SessionExport now redacts tool-state strings while preserving tool identity. SessionExport.sanitizedPart redacts pending raw input text, running/completed titles, completed output, and error messages for tool parts. SessionPersistenceSmoke verifies raw read-tool output/title remain in unsanitized export and sanitized export replaces output/title with stable redaction markers while preserving callID/status structure. Docs and matrix now call out tool-output sanitization.
Additional partial evidence: SessionPersistenceSmoke now covers sanitized export for failed tool states. The fixture persists a missing-file read tool turn, verifies raw export keeps error status and diagnostic text, and verifies sanitized export preserves callID/status while redacting the tool error string with a stable redaction marker. Docs and matrix now call out tool-output/error sanitization.
Additional partial evidence and privacy fix: sanitized SessionExport now redacts tool-state input payloads as well as output/error strings. SessionExport.sanitizedPart replaces dynamic tool input with a stable redaction marker for all tool states while preserving callID/tool/status/timing structure. SessionPersistenceSmoke verifies unsanitized success/error exports retain read tool filePath inputs, and sanitized exports redact those inputs for both completed and errored tool states. Docs and matrix now call out tool input/output/error sanitization.
Additional partial evidence: the CLI now wires the first non-interactive upstream export path. Cli.export <sessionID> resolves the configured SQLite database through StorageDatabasePath/OPENCODE_DB, reads SessionExport data through the SessionStore seam, writes JSON-only stdout, preserves the upstream-style progress line on stderr, supports --sanitize, and reports missing sessions. CliSmoke seeds a temp SqliteSessionStore and verifies raw export JSON, sanitized text redaction, stderr separation, and missing-session failure.
Additional partial evidence: run --session now has first non-interactive recovery wiring. The deterministic and mock CLI run paths validate a provided session ID through StorageDatabasePath/OPENCODE_DB and SessionProcessor.recover, reuse the recovered session ID, and default the run directory from the stored session unless --dir overrides it. CliSmoke verifies recovered session ID, recovered directory, explicit directory override, missing-session failure, --fork validation, and keeps --continue explicitly deferred until latest-session lookup/history-aware continuation lands.
Additional partial evidence: run --continue now has latest-root selection. SessionStore exposes listSessions(limit), SqliteSessionStore returns sessions by time_updated desc/id desc, and Cli.run --continue selects the newest root session while skipping forked children before using the same recovery path as --session. StorageSmoke covers newest-first session listing; CliSmoke seeds a newer child/fork session, verifies --continue chooses the root session, and keeps --fork explicitly deferred.
Additional partial evidence: new non-resumed headless runs now persist when OPENCODE_DB is explicitly configured. Cli.run opens the configured SqliteSessionStore only for fresh sessions, generates a fresh ses_... ID from node:crypto randomUUID, passes the store into SessionProcessor, and closes it after the run. The mock AI SDK run path is wired the same way for fresh sessions. CliSmoke and cli-smoke.mjs verify a generated session can be exported immediately with two persisted messages. Resumed-session append persistence remains deferred because deterministic message IDs would overwrite existing stored messages until broader history-aware message ID generation lands.
Additional partial evidence: OPENCODE_DB-backed resumed headless runs now append persisted turns instead of only validating recovery. Cli.run/run --mock-ai-sdk pass a fresh turn_... seed and current timestamp into SessionProcessor for recovered sessions, so user/assistant message IDs and part IDs no longer collide with prior stored turns. SessionProcessor preserves deterministic fixture IDs when no turn seed is provided, preserves the original stored session directory while allowing per-message --dir path metadata, and updates the session timestamp. CliSmoke and cli-smoke.mjs verify a generated session exports two messages, a following run --session appends another two-message turn, and export returns all four messages in order. Full history-aware prompt construction remains deferred.
Additional partial evidence: OPENCODE_DB-backed persistence/resume append is now explicitly covered on the async mock AI SDK CLI path, not only the deterministic fake-provider path. CliSmoke.runAsync and scripts/harness/cli-smoke.mjs run generated/headless run --mock-ai-sdk with a configured SQLite store, export the fresh two-message session, resume it with --session, and verify export returns four messages. This proves the generated CLI entrypoint, Cli.runAsync, SessionProcessor.runAiSdk, and SessionStore persistence cooperate without credentials; real live-provider history-aware continuation remains deferred.

title: Refresh residual parity matrix ownership
description:
[discovered-from: opencodehx-000]

The seed backlog is closed, but reference/opencode-test-port-matrix.csv still contains partial/deferred rows whose next_bead values point at now-closed seed tasks or the parent epic. Create active ownership for the remaining parity rows so bd ready reflects real next work instead of a closeable epic.
acceptance_criteria: Every partial/deferred executable row in the generated test port matrix either points to an open follow-up Bead, is promoted to direct/ported with evidence, or has an explicit no-follow-up rationale; scripts/inventory/build-parity-matrix.mjs, docs/opencode-test-port-matrix.md, reference/opencode-test-port-matrix.csv, README progress, and Beads are synchronized; public precommit and diff checks pass.
close_reason: accepted: regenerated the test port matrix from scripts/inventory/build-parity-matrix.mjs with active M11 next_bead owners; added opencodehx-000.11 for residual foundation/runtime rows; no partial/deferred executable rows point at opencodehx-000 or closed seed owners; npm run inventory:matrix, npm run public:precommit, and git diff --check passed.

title: Installed serve exercises PTY WebSocket
description: Extend the npm global package smoke so the installed opencodehx serve binary proves PTY HTTP routes and the /pty/:id/connect WebSocket against a real listener.
acceptance_criteria: package:smoke installs the packed tarball, starts serve, creates a cat-backed PTY, writes through WebSocket, verifies echoed output and replay/tail cursor behavior, deletes the PTY, documents the installed PTY boundary, and passes local gates.
close_reason: accepted: package:smoke now installs the packed tarball, starts serve, creates a cat-backed PTY, verifies WebSocket write output, replay from cursor=0, tail from cursor=-1, and PTY delete from the installed binary. Gates: npm run package:smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.

title: Installed serve streams session events
description: Extend the npm global package smoke so the installed opencodehx serve binary proves the /event SSE stream from a real listener, including the session.created event emitted by an installed session workflow.
acceptance_criteria: package:smoke installs the packed tarball, starts serve, opens /event, creates a session, observes server.connected and session.created for that session, documents the installed SSE boundary, and passes local gates.
close_reason: accepted: package:smoke now starts the installed serve binary, opens /event, observes server.connected plus live session.created for the created session, and still verifies /health, session list/message/select/abort. Gates: npm run package:smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.

title: Installed serve exercises session workflow
description: Extend the npm global package smoke beyond /health so the installed opencodehx binary starts serve and proves the in-memory session workflow through HTTP routes.
acceptance_criteria: package:smoke installs the packed tarball, starts serve, creates a session, lists sessions, reads a message page, selects the session through the TUI route, aborts it, and documents the remaining installed-server boundary.
close_reason: accepted: package:smoke now starts the installed serve binary and verifies /health, session create/list, message page read, TUI select-session, and abort; docs describe the installed-server boundary.

title: Installed package includes runnable TUI scaffold
description: Tighten installed TUI packaging evidence without claiming full live TUI parity. The package smoke should build the existing TUI scaffold before npm pack, include the generated TSX and a packaged OpenTUI/Solid preload, install the tarball, and run the scaffold through the package-local Bun binary.
acceptance_criteria: package:smoke builds the TUI scaffold before packing; packed files include src-gen/tui/index.tsx and the packaged preload; the globally installed package runs src-gen/tui/index.tsx with package-local Bun and the packaged preload and prints tui-scaffold:ok; npm-global-packaging/tui docs and README mention installed TUI scaffold evidence; normal gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: package:smoke now builds the TUI scaffold before packing; packed files include src-gen/tui TSX and a packaged OpenTUI/Solid preload; bun is a runtime dependency for package-local installed TUI scaffold execution; package smoke installs the tarball and runs src-gen/tui/index.tsx through installed Bun with the packaged preload. Gates: npm run package:smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.

title: Installed package run supports workspace dir
description: Tighten bootstrapping evidence for the globally installed opencodehx binary: the deterministic run and mock AI SDK run paths should honor --dir so installed CLI smokes prove a real workspace path rather than only the fixed transcript fixture directory. Keep default transcript behavior stable for existing parity oracles.
acceptance_criteria: Cli.run/Cli.runAsync honor --dir for deterministic and --mock-ai-sdk run paths; package smoke installs the tarball, runs both paths with a temp workspace, and verifies JSON transcript assistant paths use that workspace; docs mention installed run --dir/mock evidence; build/package smoke/ci gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: deterministic run and mock AI SDK run now honor --dir; CliSmoke covers workspace path metadata and missing directory errors; package smoke verifies installed run --dir and installed run --mock-ai-sdk --dir transcripts from a temp global install; docs updated. Gates: npm run cli:smoke; npm run package:smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.

title: Fix CI bootstrap for Bun and genes checkout
description: GitHub CI failed after the Windows shell parity slice because jobs used npm ci --ignore-scripts without installing the local Bun binary before Bun-backed harnesses, and the formatter job ran lix download without the sibling ../genes checkout required by genes-ts.hxml.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow checks out genes where lix/Haxe needs ../genes; Node smoke rebuilds better-sqlite3 after --ignore-scripts before StorageSmoke exercises SQLite; Bun-backed CI gates install the local bun binary after --ignore-scripts; Node smoke installs/proves ripgrep before FileSmoke exercises the rg seam; Windows shell harness imports generated modules through file URLs on Windows.
close_reason: accepted: GitHub CI run 27996552816 passed after node-smoke checked out genes-ts, rebuilt better-sqlite3 after npm ci --ignore-scripts, installed ripgrep before FileSmoke, installed the local bun binary before Bun-backed tests, and windows-shell-parity passed with file-URL imports. Local gates passed: npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; npm rebuild better-sqlite3; git diff --check.

title: Move unknown narrowing primitives into genes-ts
description: OpenCodeHX has a temporary opencodehx.interop.UnknownAccess quarantine for TypeScript unknown narrowing at JSON/JS boundaries. The reusable primitive belongs in ../genes as generic genes.ts.UnknownNarrow/UnknownRecord/UnknownArray support, with no OpenCodeHX-specific knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: A paired genes-ts task exists or ../genes Beads is bootstrapped; genes-ts exposes generic unknown narrowing helpers; OpenCodeHX replaces the local transitional UnknownAccess with genes.ts helpers; generated TS for decoders remains strict, readable, and avoids scattered Register.unsafeCast/Syntax.code in product modules.
notes: GPT 5.5 Pro recommendation: genes-ts owns Unknown, UnknownNarrow, UnknownRecord, read-only UnknownArray, and clean compiler lowering; OpenCodeHX owns domain decoders. Local UnknownAccess should stay temporary and not become canonical.
close_reason: accepted: genes-ts now exposes generic UnknownNarrow/UnknownRecord/UnknownArray helpers in ../genes commit ed61fe7; OpenCodeHX removed the local UnknownAccess/UnknownRecord quarantine and migrated Npm, SkillRemoteDiscovery, InstallationRuntime, and SyncRouteRuntime to genes.ts helpers. Generated decoder scan shows no local helper, scattered unknown unsafeCast, or old raw assertion patterns. Gates passed: npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.

title: Port exact Copilot LanguageModelV3 facade and responses model
description: The registry can now resolve configured @ai-sdk/github-copilot chat models into the Haxe-owned CopilotChatLanguageModel through ProviderRegistry.resolveCopilotChat, but that class is still typed around OpenCodeHX Copilot request DTOs rather than the exact AI SDK LanguageModelV3 call-options contract. Do not cast the Haxe model to LanguageModelV3. Port the exact facade honestly, add responses-model support for upstream shouldUseCopilotResponsesApi routing, and keep generated TS strict and readable.
acceptance_criteria: ProviderRegistry/getLanguage can route GitHub Copilot models through a Haxe-owned implementation that is structurally accepted as LanguageModelV3 by TypeScript; gpt-5 non-mini models route to a ported responses model; chat models keep current CopilotChatLanguageModel behavior; upstream-shaped Copilot provider tests or equivalent Haxe smokes cover chat vs responses selection; no broad Dynamic/cast workaround is introduced.
notes: Accepted: Copilot Responses LanguageModelV3 path is now ported for gpt-5 non-mini routing. ProviderRegistry.getLanguage selects chat vs responses through CopilotLanguageLoader, CopilotResponsesLanguageModel implements doGenerate/doStream over typed request DTOs, shared SDK prompt conversion is centralized, and Responses HTTP/JSON/SSE boundaries are localized and documented. Evidence: npm run ci:full passed; npm run public:precommit passed; CopilotResponsesLanguageModelSmoke covers gpt-5 responses routing, /responses request bodies, developer-message/reasoning behavior, non-stream text/source/tool-call mapping, core SSE mapping, and getLanguage chat-vs-responses selection.
close_reason: accepted: Copilot chat and Responses LanguageModelV3 routing/facades are ported with Haxe-owned smokes; npm run ci:full and npm run public:precommit pass.

title: Replace raw NodeHonoAdapter Syntax.code lifecycle
description: Refactor NodeHonoAdapter away from a large js.Syntax.code Promise block into typed Haxe/extern calls. Add AGENTS guidance that Syntax.code is a last-resort interop escape hatch requiring nearby justification, and scan existing uses for follow-up cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: NodeHonoAdapter.listen is implemented as proper Haxe over narrow externs; any remaining Syntax.code in the touched path has a nearby justification; AGENTS.md has the hard rule; repo scan identifies remaining Syntax.code follow-ups; relevant build/smoke gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: replaced the large NodeHonoAdapter Syntax.code Promise lifecycle with Haxe over typed Hono/NodeServer externs; Hono fetch is typed; header/json server helpers now use HonoContext; parsePtyCursor is Haxe; remaining server Syntax.code uses are localized SSE/PTY boundaries with comments. Follow-up opencodehx-1le tracks repo-wide Syntax.code audit. Gate: npm run ci:full.

title: Provider SDK loading and transform parity
description: Follow-up after the first AI SDK streamText smoke. Port real provider SDK loading/getLanguageModel paths, provider-specific request options, upstream transform variants, models.dev cache/fetch behavior, and plugin provider hooks against upstream provider tests.
acceptance_criteria: At least one credential-backed or locally mocked real provider loading path exercises getLanguageModel through the registry; provider transforms and provider.test.ts/model discovery cases have Haxe-owned or differential fixtures; CI remains credential-optional.
notes: Progress: cleaned up the ProviderRegistry env host seam. ProviderRegistry no longer imports js.Syntax or embeds raw process.env spread code; default env loading now goes through host.node.NodeProcess.env(), keeping Node process access centralized in the host layer while preserving injected-env testability. Docs were updated with the seam rationale. Gates passed: npm run build; npm run smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.
close_reason: accepted: real credential-free AI SDK provider loading now routes through ProviderRegistry/getLanguage and SessionProcessor, provider transforms and models.dev discovery/cache/fetch have Haxe-owned fixtures, bundled provider SDK loaders cover OpenAI-compatible/OpenAI/xAI/Azure/Google/Vertex/Anthropic/Bedrock/Mistral/Groq/Cohere/Perplexity/OpenRouter/DeepInfra/Cerebras/Gateway/TogetherAI/Vercel/Alibaba/GitLab plus Cloudflare AI Gateway, and CI remains credential-optional. Remaining live credentials, dynamic/non-bundled provider installation, plugin hooks, and deeper provider cases are deferred to dedicated follow-up Beads.

title: genes-ts enum abstract array literals widen to string in helper loops
description:
[discovered-from: opencodehx-obx]

A Haxe helper that iterated an array of closed enum abstract values and passed each item to a function expecting that enum abstract generated TypeScript locals typed as plain string. Under strict TypeScript this failed because the callee expected the closed literal union. OpenCodeHX worked around this by appending each known service ID explicitly in InstanceBootstrapRuntime.upstreamOrder, but genes-ts should preserve the closed enum abstract union through array literals and loop locals generically.

Reduced shape: closed enum abstract ServiceID(String) with values config/plugin/etc; function noop(id:ServiceID); for (id in [Config, Plugin]) noop(id). Generated TS should not emit id: string when the array members form the ServiceID union.
acceptance_criteria: A generic genes-ts fixture covers closed enum abstract values inside array literals and loop locals; generated TS preserves the literal union expected by typed callees; OpenCodeHX can remove the explicit-append workaround without typecheck failures.
close_reason: accepted: genes-ts fix landed in ../genes and remote genes-ts CI passed; OpenCodeHX removed InstanceBootstrapRuntime explicit service append workaround; npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run ci:full, and npm run public:precommit pass

title: Port live workspace HTTP/SSE and proxy adapters
description: Follow-up from opencodehx-kcp. The deterministic workspace sync loop covers /sync/start delegation, active-session history pull, local history replay, upstream-shaped SSE frame parsing, workspace-global event forwarding, fence checks, reconnect delay math, and per-event replay failure diagnostics. The live remote side-effect seams now cover real fetch/ReadableStream connection to /global/event, authenticated /sync/history and /sync/replay HTTP requests, abort forwarding, bounded reconnect loop planning, daemon-style task timer/abort ownership, and deterministic workspace HTTP proxy/fence behavior. Broader workspace control-plane route/service integration remains in later server/workspace slices.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted: narrow host seams cover real remote workspace HTTP/SSE connection, headers, abort handling, reconnect scheduling, and history/replay HTTP failure bodies; daemon-style task ownership is represented with deterministic timer/abort fixtures; workspace proxy/fence behavior is represented with Haxe-owned fixtures; normal smoke remains deterministic; docs and matrix distinguish live side-effect coverage from broader workspace service routing; generated TS strict-checks and public precommit pass.
notes:
Progress: added WorkspaceSyncRemoteHttp as a typed remote workspace HTTP seam with injected fetch support. The seam normalizes base URLs for /global/event, /sync/history, and /sync/replay; preserves caller headers; sets JSON content-type for history/replay POSTs; forwards AbortSignal; decodes /sync/history response rows through SyncRouteRuntime.decodeHistoryEvents; decodes /sync/replay sessionID responses; and throws WorkspaceSyncHttpError with status/body text for remote history/replay failures. ServerSmoke covers the seam with a fake fetch, including URL cleanup, auth/header preservation, request bodies, signal forwarding, response decoding, SSE body presence, and HTTP failure diagnostics.

Progress: added WorkspaceSyncSse.parseStream for chunked ReadableStream<Uint8Array> SSE parsing. It preserves incomplete frame buffers across chunks, emits only complete SSE frames, stops when the supplied AbortSignal is already aborted, cancels the reader, and feeds WorkspaceSyncRuntime.applyRemoteSseStream. ServerSmoke proves split-frame replay, incomplete trailing-frame suppression, and connected-to-disconnected status transitions after stream end.

Progress: added WorkspaceSyncRuntime.runRemoteLoop, a bounded deterministic loop that connects through WorkspaceSyncRemoteHttp, syncs remote history using active-session known seqs, applies the SSE stream, records connected/disconnected/error statuses, captures failures, and records the reconnect delay it would schedule. ServerSmoke covers connect-before-history ordering, known-seq history body, history+stream event application, final disconnect state, and first planned reconnect delay.

Progress: added WorkspaceSyncBackgroundTask as the daemon-style workspace sync side-effect owner. The task owns an AbortControllerWithReason, dedupes start(), schedules the first tick through an injected scheduler or real WebTimers.setTimeout/clearTimeout scheduler, runs one bounded remote-loop tick per wakeup, forwards its owned AbortSignal into remote HTTP calls, records reconnect delay scheduling from the runtime result, and stop() aborts and clears a pending timer. ServerSmoke injects a deterministic scheduler/fetch to prove immediate tick scheduling, start dedupe, owned fetch signal forwarding, reconnect scheduling, and stop cleanup without remote infrastructure.

Progress: added WorkspaceProxy as the deterministic workspace HTTP proxy seam. It covers upstream-shaped local session-route classification, target URL/query rewriting with workspace query removal, WebSocket URL scheme rewriting, hop-by-hop/header cleanup, target header injection, POST body forwarding, response content-length/content-encoding cleanup, disconnected workspace 503 behavior, and x-opencode-sync fence parsing/waiting through WorkspaceSyncRuntime with 504 timeout diagnostics. ServerSmoke covers these without remote infrastructure.

Accepted gates: npm run format:haxe && npm run build && npm run smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.
close_reason: accepted: live workspace HTTP/SSE and proxy adapter seams are covered. Added typed remote HTTP/SSE fetch seams for /global/event, /sync/history, and /sync/replay with headers, abort signals, response decoding, and failure bodies; chunked SSE stream parsing; bounded reconnect-loop planning; daemon-style background task timer/abort ownership; and WorkspaceProxy HTTP behavior for local-route classification, URL/query rewrite, WebSocket scheme rewrite, header cleanup/injection, body forwarding, response content-header cleanup, disconnected workspace 503, and x-opencode-sync fence success/timeout diagnostics. Normal smoke remains deterministic and docs/matrix were updated. Gates passed: npm run format:haxe && npm run build && npm run smoke; npm run ci:full; npm run public:precommit; git diff --check.
